251.name: MFI(10) >50 + Median (cc3_252.py)
251.description: A trend-following strategy that combines a long-term price median filter with a short-period Money Flow Index. QQQ is used as the signal asset and TQQQ/BIL as execution vehicles. When both the median filter and MFI agree, the strategy goes fully long TQQQ; partial disagreement yields a 50/50 split.
251.overfit_notes: MFI period of 10 and threshold of 50 are both relatively standard; the 200-day median split (index 100 of sorted closes) is a slightly unusual trend gate but not aggressively tuned. Overfit risk is low.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit (MR)
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

252.name: MFI(20) >60 + Median (cc3_253.py)
252.description: A trend-following strategy pairing a 200-day price median filter on QQQ with a 20-period MFI threshold. Raises the MFI bar to 60 versus the standard 50 midline, requiring more positive money flow pressure before entering TQQQ. Partial agreement produces a 50/50 TQQQ/BIL allocation.
252.overfit_notes: MFI threshold of 60 is a one-notch tightening above the neutral midline; the 20-period window is slightly non-standard but close to the textbook 14. Two components with one lightly tuned threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

253.name: MFI(14) >50 70/30 middle (cc3_254.py)
253.description: A trend-following strategy combining the standard 14-period MFI with a 200-day price median filter. Uniquely uses a 70/30 TQQQ/BIL split in the mixed state (when only one of the two conditions is met), leaning more aggressively toward the leveraged ETF than the equal-weight variant.
253.overfit_notes: MFI(14) at threshold 50 is canonical; the 70/30 mixed-state allocation is a tuned parameter with no fundamental justification. Two components, one lightly tuned allocation weight.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

254.name: MFI(14) >40 (loose) (cc3_255.py)
254.description: A loosened variant of the MFI median trend-follower, using a below-neutral MFI threshold of 40 to allow entry under weaker money-flow conditions. This makes the strategy more permissive: it enters TQQQ whenever the price is above its median and even modest positive flow is present.
254.overfit_notes: MFI(14) threshold of 40 is deliberately loose and below the neutral 50 midline, reducing the strength filter's selectivity. The 50/50 mixed allocation is standard. One slightly tuned threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

255.name: MFI(30) >50 60/40 middle (cc3_256.py)
255.description: A trend-following strategy using a slower 30-period MFI paired with the 200-day price median filter on QQQ. The mixed state tilts slightly toward TQQQ at a 60/40 split, giving a moderate bias toward the trend in ambiguous conditions.
255.overfit_notes: MFI period of 30 is longer than canonical; threshold of 50 is standard. The 60/40 mixed-state split is a mildly tuned allocation. Two standard components with one lightly tuned weight.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 60% TQQQ / 40% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

256.name: Stochastic + Williams %R + Median (cc3_257.py)
256.description: A trend-following strategy that requires consensus from two separate momentum oscillators — Stochastic and Williams %R — in addition to a long-term price median filter. Both oscillators must signal bullish conditions simultaneously for a full TQQQ position; partial agreement yields a 50/50 split.
256.overfit_notes: Stochastic(14,3,3) and Williams %R(14) are at canonical periods; thresholds of StochK > StochD (no level threshold) and W%R > -50 (midline) are standard. Three components total but all at near-textbook settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Trend gate AND both oscillators bullish → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One of {trend, oscillator consensus} true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither → 100% BIL

257.name: PSAR + Median (cc3_258.py)
257.description: A trend-following strategy combining Parabolic SAR with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ. Price above the PSAR dot indicates an uptrend; when this aligns with the median filter, the strategy goes fully into TQQQ. Disagreement between the two produces a 50/50 split.
257.overfit_notes: PSAR uses default parameters (step=0.02, initial=0.02, max=0.2), which are textbook. The 200-day median filter is a standard long-term gate. Two standard components at canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

258.name: CCI + Median (cc3_259.py)
258.description: A trend-following strategy combining a 20-period Commodity Channel Index with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ. CCI above zero indicates bullish momentum; paired with the median trend gate, this drives a three-state TQQQ/BIL allocation.
258.overfit_notes: CCI(20) is a standard period; the threshold of zero (neutral line) rather than the conventional ±100 overbought/oversold levels is a slight but reasonable deviation. Two components, one lightly non-canonical threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

259.name: LR Forecast 30 + Median (cc3_260.py)
259.description: A trend-following strategy that pairs a 200-day price median filter with a hand-computed 30-bar linear regression forecast. The forecast is bullish when the extrapolated next-bar price exceeds the current price by more than a small negative buffer, signaling upward slope. Execution is binary: fully TQQQ or fully BIL.
259.overfit_notes: The 30-bar regression window is a custom parameter; the 0.99 price tolerance buffer (forecast > price * 0.99) is a lightly tuned threshold. Two components, both somewhat non-standard but not extreme.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

260.name: Williams %R(50) + Median (cc3_261.py)
260.description: A trend-following strategy using an extended 50-period Williams %R oscillator alongside a 200-day price median filter. The W%R midline crossing (above -50) acts as the momentum gate. Three-state allocation switches between full TQQQ, a 50/50 mix, and full BIL.
260.overfit_notes: Williams %R period of 50 is longer than the standard 14; threshold of -50 is the standard midline. One slightly non-canonical period, one standard threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

261.name: ADX(14) > 25 + DI+ + Median (cc3_262.py)
261.description: A trend-following strategy gating entry on both ADX trend strength and directional bias. A combined bullish signal requires ADX to exceed 25 (indicating a trending market) and DI+ to be above DI- (confirming upward direction), plus the 200-day median filter. The mixed state uses a 60/40 TQQQ/BIL split.
261.overfit_notes: ADX(14) at threshold 25 is the classic textbook definition of a trending market; DI+ > DI- is standard directional logic. The 60/40 mixed-state allocation is a light tuning. Near-canonical parameters overall.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 60% TQQQ / 40% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

262.name: ADX(30) + Median (cc3_263.py)
262.description: A trend-following strategy combining a slower 30-period ADX with a directional filter and the 200-day price median. ADX strength is checked at the lower threshold of 20, and DI+ must exceed DI- to confirm a bullish trend. Three-state allocation uses equal 50/50 in the mixed state.
262.overfit_notes: ADX period of 30 is non-standard (vs canonical 14); the ADX threshold of 20 (instead of the textbook 25) is a slight adjustment. DI+ > DI- is standard. Two mildly non-canonical parameters.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

263.name: DI+ > DI- only + Median (cc3_264.py)
263.description: A stripped-down directional-indicator trend follower that uses only the DI+ vs DI- relationship from ADX (ignoring the ADX level itself), combined with a 200-day price median filter. This tests whether directional bias alone, without a trend-strength gate, is sufficient for timing TQQQ.
263.overfit_notes: Using only DI+/DI- without an ADX level threshold is a simplified but legitimate approach; ADX(14) at canonical period. The 200-day median is standard. Two standard components with no unusual thresholds.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

264.name: ADX+MFI+Median 4-state (cc3_265.py)
264.description: A four-state trend-following strategy that counts independent bullish votes from three sources: a 200-day price median filter, DI+ > DI- from ADX, and MFI above 50. The count (0–3) directly maps to a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation from 0% to 100%.
264.overfit_notes: Three components (median, ADX directional, MFI), all at near-canonical settings. The 4-state step-down allocation table (1.0/0.7/0.3/0.0) is a parameterized choice; the concept is standard. Moderate overfit risk from the explicit allocation steps.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

265.name: DI Cross (DI+/DI-) (cc3_266.py)
265.description: A signal-triggered trend follower that enters TQQQ only on the actual crossover of DI+ above DI- (not merely the current state) when the 200-day median filter is bullish. Exit is triggered on the reverse crossover (DI- crosses above DI+) regardless of the median.
265.overfit_notes: Uses ADX(14) at canonical period; DI crossover is a standard entry concept. The median filter on entry but not on exit introduces slight asymmetry. No unusual thresholds.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (entry only)

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: DI+(14) crosses above DI-(14) AND in trend → 100% TQQQ

266.name: Tenkan/Kijun Cross + Median (cc3_267.py)
266.description: A trend-following strategy using the Ichimoku Tenkan-sen (9-period midpoint) versus Kijun-sen (26-period midpoint) relationship as a momentum signal, combined with a 200-day price median filter. When Tenkan is above Kijun (a standard bullish Ichimoku signal) and the median gate passes, the strategy holds TQQQ.
266.overfit_notes: Tenkan (9) and Kijun (26) periods are the textbook Ichimoku defaults; the 200-day median is standard. All components at canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

267.name: Above Tenkan+Kijun 4-state (cc3_268.py)
267.description: A four-state Ichimoku trend follower that counts three independent bullish conditions: the 200-day median filter, price above Tenkan-sen, and price above Kijun-sen. Each passing condition adds one vote, driving a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation across four levels.
267.overfit_notes: Ichimoku periods (9, 26) are canonical. The 4-state allocation table (1.0/0.7/0.3/0.0) is a parameterized design choice; using price relative to Tenkan and Kijun separately (rather than as a cross) is somewhat non-standard.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Price > Tenkan (9-period midpoint) (1 vote); Price > Kijun (26-period midpoint) (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

268.name: Above Ichimoku Cloud (cc3_269.py)
268.description: A trend-following strategy requiring QQQ to trade above the full Ichimoku cloud (Senkou Span A and B computed from standard 9/26/52 periods) alongside a 200-day price median filter. Being above both cloud boundaries is a classically strong bullish Ichimoku signal.
268.overfit_notes: Ichimoku periods (9, 26, 52) are all standard textbook values. The cloud-top condition is canonical. All components at unmodified defaults.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Price > max(Senkou Span A, Senkou Span B) — above entire Ichimoku cloud

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

269.name: Stochastic(50,5,5) + Median (cc3_270.py)
269.description: A trend-following strategy using a slow, wide 50-period Stochastic oscillator above its midline (50) as a momentum filter, combined with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ. The extra-long Stochastic period dampens noise and provides a slower-turning momentum signal.
269.overfit_notes: Stochastic period of 50 with smoothing (5,5) is significantly non-standard versus the canonical (14,3,3); threshold of %K > 50 is the midline, which is standard. One notably non-canonical period choice.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

270.name: Up/Down Vol Ratio + Median (cc3_271.py)
270.description: A trend-following strategy using an up/down volume ratio as a market breadth filter: summing volume on up-days versus down-days over the past 20 sessions and requiring buyers to dominate. Combined with a 200-day price median filter on QQQ, it drives a three-state TQQQ/BIL allocation.
270.overfit_notes: The 20-day lookback window and the ratio threshold of 1.0 are both round numbers and reasonable defaults. Two standard-feeling components, though the up/down volume ratio is less common than MFI.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Sum of volume on up-days / sum of volume on down-days (last 20 days) > 1.0

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

271.name: QQQ vs TLT + Median (cc3_272.py)
271.description: A cross-asset trend follower that uses relative performance of QQQ versus long-term Treasuries (TLT) over a 60-day window as a risk-on/risk-off signal. When equities are outperforming bonds on a 3-month basis and QQQ is above its long-term price median, the strategy holds TQQQ.
271.overfit_notes: The 60-day relative performance lookback and the 200-day median are both round standard parameters. QQQ vs TLT relative momentum is a well-known risk-on signal. Low overfit risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + TLT. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

272.name: QQQ vs SHY rates calm + Median (cc3_273.py)
272.description: A cross-asset trend follower that monitors short-term Treasury ETF (SHY) performance as a rate-calm indicator. When SHY has not lost more than 0.5% over the past 60 days (rates are not spiking) and QQQ is above its 200-day median, the strategy holds TQQQ.
272.overfit_notes: The SHY 60-day return threshold of -0.005 is a specific, somewhat tuned parameter. The concept of using SHY as a rates-calm filter is sensible but the exact threshold introduces modest overfit risk. Two components with one tuned threshold.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + SHY. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

273.name: Weak Dollar + Median (cc3_274.py)
273.description: A cross-asset trend follower that uses the US dollar ETF (UUP) as a macro filter: requiring the dollar to be weak (below a 2% gain over 60 days) before entering TQQQ alongside the 200-day QQQ median filter. A weakening dollar is historically associated with risk-on equity rallies.
273.overfit_notes: The UUP 60-day return threshold of 2% (< 0.02) is a specific tuned value rather than zero; it allows mild dollar strength but not strong rallies. One moderately tuned threshold. The 200-day median is standard.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + UUP. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

274.name: HYG vs IEI Credit + Median (cc3_275.py)
274.description: A cross-asset trend follower using credit market risk appetite as a signal: high-yield bonds (HYG) outperforming intermediate Treasuries (IEI) over 60 days indicates risk-on conditions. Combined with the 200-day QQQ median filter, this drives a three-state TQQQ/BIL allocation.
274.overfit_notes: HYG vs IEI relative performance is a well-known credit spread proxy; the 60-day window is a round standard parameter. The zero-return threshold (relative return comparison) needs no tuning. Low overfit risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + HYG + IEI. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

275.name: SPY Range Position + Median (cc3_276.py)
275.description: A cross-asset trend follower that measures SPY's position within its 50-day price range as a market-strength gauge. When SPY is in the upper 30% of its 50-day range and QQQ is above its 200-day median, the strategy holds TQQQ. This captures periods when the broad market is near its recent highs.
275.overfit_notes: The 50-day range lookback and the 0.7 range-position threshold are both tuned parameters. Using SPY range position as a secondary signal (while executing on TQQQ) adds specificity. Two components, both with somewhat tuned parameters.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ + SPY. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

276.name: Triple MFI consensus + Median (cc3_277.py)
276.description: A multi-timeframe money-flow trend follower that runs three MFI periods simultaneously (7, 14, 30 days) and counts how many exceed 50, then combines that count with a 200-day median filter for a four-level TQQQ/BIL allocation. Requiring consensus across short, medium, and long money-flow windows filters out noise.
276.overfit_notes: Three MFI periods (7, 14, 30) and their thresholds of 50 are all at or near standard values; the specific allocation steps (1.0/0.6/0.3/0.0) and the partial consensus logic (in_trend and n>=1 vs in_trend or n>=2) add tuned complexity. Moderate overfit risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: Trend + all 3 MFI → 100% TQQQ; trend + ≥1 MFI → 60% TQQQ / 40% BIL; trend or ≥2 MFI → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; else → 100% BIL

277.name: MFI(5) + Median (cc3_278.py)
277.description: A fast-reacting trend-following strategy using a very short 5-period MFI as a near-term money-flow gate alongside the 200-day QQQ median filter. The short MFI period makes this more reactive to daily volume shifts, potentially increasing turnover compared to longer-period variants.
277.overfit_notes: MFI period of 5 is notably short compared to the canonical 14; threshold of 50 is standard. One non-canonical period parameter. The 200-day median and 50/50 split are standard.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

278.name: MFI(50) + Median (cc3_279.py)
278.description: A slow-turning trend-following strategy using a very long 50-period MFI to capture sustained money flow trends, combined with the 200-day QQQ price median filter. The extended MFI period reduces false signals but also slows response to trend changes.
278.overfit_notes: MFI period of 50 is significantly longer than the canonical 14; threshold of 50 is the standard midline. One notably non-canonical period choice. The 200-day median is standard.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

279.name: MFI Rising + Median (cc3_280.py)
279.description: A trend-following strategy requiring MFI to be both above 50 and rising (higher than it was 10 days ago), filtering for accelerating positive money flow alongside the 200-day QQQ median. This adds a momentum-of-momentum condition to the standard MFI filter.
279.overfit_notes: MFI(14) at threshold 50 is canonical; the 10-day lookback for trend direction is a mildly tuned parameter. Requiring both level and rising direction adds one additional condition. Low-to-moderate overfit.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: MFI(14) > 50 AND MFI is higher than 10 days ago (rising)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

280.name: MFI+W%R+Median 4-state (cc3_281.py)
280.description: A four-state trend follower combining the 200-day price median, MFI above 50, and Williams %R above -50 into a vote-counting framework. Each indicator contributes one vote; the total (0–3) maps to a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation.
280.overfit_notes: MFI(14) and Williams %R(14) are at canonical periods; thresholds of 50 and -50 are the standard midlines for each. The 4-state allocation table (1.0/0.7/0.3/0.0) is a parameterized design. Three components at standard settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

281.name: Range Compressed 80% (cc3_282.py)
281.description: A volatility-based trend follower that uses recent price-range compression as a buy signal: when QQQ's average daily range over the past 25 days is less than 80% of the 200-day average range (a volatility squeeze), and price is above its median, the strategy holds TQQQ. Compressed ranges often precede breakouts.
281.overfit_notes: The 25-day recent range window and the 0.8 compression threshold are both tuned parameters; the 200-day baseline is standard. The threshold of 80% is a specific choice with no canonical basis, introducing moderate overfit.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) < 80% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

282.name: Range Compressed 90% (cc3_283.py)
282.description: A looser version of the range-compression trend follower, triggering when QQQ's recent 25-day average range is below 90% of the 200-day historical average. Compared to the 80% variant, this fires more frequently as it accepts more moderately compressed conditions.
282.overfit_notes: The 90% threshold is one variant in a sweep of range-compression thresholds; no canonical justification. The 25-day/200-day window pair is a design choice. Similar overfit profile to the 80% variant.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) < 90% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

283.name: Range Expanded 110% (cc3_284.py)
283.description: A volatility-expansion trend follower that enters TQQQ when QQQ's recent 25-day average range exceeds 110% of the 200-day average, indicating elevated volatility, combined with a median trend gate. This tests whether periods of rising volatility in an uptrend produce strong forward returns.
283.overfit_notes: The 110% threshold inverts the compression logic; it is one step in a family of threshold sweeps. Elevated volatility entry is a non-standard signal direction compared to breakout compression strategies. Moderate tuning.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 110% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

284.name: Range Expanded 120% (cc3_285.py)
284.description: A variant of the range-expansion trend follower with a tighter expansion requirement: the recent 25-day average range must exceed 120% of the 200-day historical average. This fires less frequently than the 110% variant and targets more pronounced volatility expansions.
284.overfit_notes: The 120% threshold is a step in the same family of range-expansion threshold sweeps. The concept is the same as the 110% variant with one adjusted parameter.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 120% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

285.name: Wide Range Up Entry (cc3_286.py)
285.description: A signal-triggered trend follower that enters TQQQ only when QQQ prints a wide bullish bar: the prior session's range must be more than 1.5 times the 10-day average and the close must be above the open. Entry requires the median trend filter to also be true; positions are held for at least 10 days before re-evaluation.
285.overfit_notes: The 1.5× range multiplier, 10-day average window, and 10-day minimum holding period are all specifically tuned parameters. The wide-up-bar event-driven entry is a non-standard signal with meaningful overfit potential.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Prior bar range > 1.5× avg of prior 10 bars AND close > open → 100% TQQQ (held for ≥10 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Median trend fails OR 10-day holding period expires without re-trigger → 100% BIL

286.name: Range Expanded 130% (cc3_287.py)
286.description: A range-expansion trend follower requiring the recent 25-day average range to exceed 130% of the 200-day historical average alongside the median trend filter. This targets significantly elevated volatility regimes where upside expansion in a trend may signal strong momentum.
286.overfit_notes: The 130% threshold is one step in a family of range threshold sweeps; same architecture as the 110%/120% variants with one parameter shifted.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 130% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

287.name: Range Expanded 140% (cc3_288.py)
287.description: A range-expansion trend follower requiring the recent 25-day average range to exceed 140% of the 200-day historical average alongside the median trend filter. At this threshold, the strategy targets only high-volatility expansion periods, making triggers relatively rare.
287.overfit_notes: The 140% threshold is a further step in the same family of range sweeps. Rarer triggers at this level increase the risk that any observed performance reflects small-sample overfitting.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 140% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

288.name: Range Expanded 150% (cc3_289.py)
288.description: The most extreme range-expansion trend follower in this series, requiring the recent 25-day average range to exceed 150% of the historical 200-day average. This fires only during very high volatility regimes in an uptrend, making it highly selective.
288.overfit_notes: The 150% threshold is the most extreme in this sweep, producing the fewest triggers. Very infrequent signals greatly increase overfit risk for any backtest result achieved at this setting.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 150% of avg daily range (last 200 days)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

289.name: Range Loose 100% (cc3_290.py)
289.description: A permissive range-expansion trend follower using a 100% threshold: the 25-day average range only needs to exceed the 200-day average (any expansion above the mean). Combined with the median filter, this is essentially always true during high-volatility uptrends.
289.overfit_notes: The 100% threshold is the baseline — the strategy enters whenever current volatility is above-average, which is a frequent condition. This is effectively a volatility-regime filter at the broadest setting. Low parameter specificity.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) > 100% of avg daily range (last 200 days) (above-average volatility)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

290.name: Range Compressed 70% (cc3_291.py)
290.description: The tightest range-compression trend follower in this series: QQQ's 25-day average range must be below 70% of its 200-day average, indicating a significant volatility squeeze before entry. Combined with the median filter, this seeks the most compressed low-volatility conditions before investing in TQQQ.
290.overfit_notes: The 70% compression threshold is the most stringent in the compression sweep, producing the fewest triggers. Rare triggers increase the risk that any backtest performance reflects small-sample overfitting.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (last 25 days) < 70% of avg daily range (last 200 days) (severe compression)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

291.name: Range+MFI 4-state (cc3_292.py)
291.description: A four-state hybrid combining the 200-day median, a range-expansion filter (25-day range > 110% of 200-day average), and MFI above 50 into a vote-counting framework. Each indicator contributes one vote; the total maps to a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation.
291.overfit_notes: Three components: median (standard), range expansion at 110% (tuned), MFI(14) > 50 (standard). The 4-state allocation table and the 110% range threshold are both specific choices. Moderate overfit from combined parameter count.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Avg daily range (25d) > 110% of avg (200d) (1 vote); MFI(14) > 50 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

292.name: 5d Momentum + Median (cc3_293.py)
292.description: A short-term momentum trend follower that requires QQQ to have a positive 5-day return (last close vs 5 bars ago) alongside the 200-day price median filter. This captures very recent near-term positive momentum as a confirmation of the long-term trend.
292.overfit_notes: 5-day momentum is a standard short-term return measure; the positive-return threshold (> 0) is the most basic. The 200-day median is standard. Two simple components at minimal tuning.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

293.name: Dual Mom 20+60 4-state (cc3_294.py)
293.description: A four-state momentum trend follower combining the 200-day price median with two separate lookback momentum measures (20-day and 60-day). All three votes drive a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation. This tests whether multi-timeframe momentum agreement (1-month and 3-month) improves timing.
293.overfit_notes: 20-day and 60-day momentum lookbacks are both standard timeframes; positive-return thresholds need no tuning. The 4-state allocation table is a parameterized design choice. Three components at near-canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 20-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 60-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

294.name: 12-mo Momentum + Median (cc3_295.py)
294.description: A long-term momentum trend follower that uses the 252-trading-day annual return of QQQ as a secondary momentum filter alongside the 200-day price median. Both indicators must be positive for a full TQQQ allocation; disagreement yields a 50/50 split.
294.overfit_notes: 252-day annual return is the standard 12-month momentum measure used in academic literature; the positive-return threshold needs no tuning. The 200-day median is standard. Two components at canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% BIL; neither true → 100% BIL

295.name: VIX-Fix Calm Entry + Median (cc3_296.py)
295.description: A volatility-filtered trend follower using the Williams VIX-Fix (a synthetic volatility measure computed from price relative to a 22-day high) as a calm/stress indicator. Entry requires low synthetic VIX (below 3%) during an uptrend; exit triggers when the VIX-Fix spikes above 8% or the trend fails.
295.overfit_notes: The 22-day high window for VIX-Fix is a standard Williams parameter; the calm threshold of 3% and exit threshold of 8% are specific tuned values with no canonical basis. Two thresholds introducing moderate overfit risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: VIX-Fix > 8% OR trend gate fails → 100% BIL

296.name: Dual Mom 10+30 4-state (cc3_297.py)
296.description: A four-state momentum trend follower using shorter 10-day and 30-day momentum lookbacks alongside the 200-day median filter. The three independent votes drive a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation, testing whether near-term and medium-term momentum agreement produces better timing.
296.overfit_notes: 10-day and 30-day momentum lookbacks are standard short and medium-term measures; positive-return thresholds need no tuning. The 4-state table is a design parameter. Three components at near-canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 10-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 30-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

297.name: Dual Mom 30+90 4-state (cc3_298.py)
297.description: A four-state momentum trend follower using medium and longer-term 30-day and 90-day momentum lookbacks alongside the 200-day median filter. The three votes drive a graduated TQQQ/BIL allocation, testing whether intermediate and quarterly momentum agreement improves timing.
297.overfit_notes: 30-day and 90-day momentum lookbacks are standard timeframes; positive-return thresholds need no tuning. The 4-state table is a design parameter. Three components at near-canonical settings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 30-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 90-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 70% TQQQ / 30% BIL; 1 vote → 30% TQQQ / 70% BIL; 0 votes → 100% BIL

298.name: Triple Mom 5-state (cc3_299.py)
298.description: A five-state momentum trend follower that counts four independent positive-return signals: the 200-day median filter, 10-day, 30-day, and 90-day momentum. The count (0–4) maps to five graduated TQQQ/BIL allocations in 25% increments. This is the most complex pure-momentum variant in this series.
298.overfit_notes: Four components (median + three momentum periods), all at near-standard settings. The 5-state allocation table with 25% steps (1.0/0.75/0.5/0.25/0.0) is a parameterized design. The specific three momentum lookbacks (10/30/90) introduce mild selection overfit.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 10-day return > 0 (1 vote); 30-day return > 0 (1 vote); 90-day return > 0 (1 vote)

299.name: Mom20+Median+Top1 (cc3_300.py)
299.description: A hybrid trend follower that pairs the standard 20-day momentum and 200-day median timing signals with a universe selection component: the single largest-cap stock from the top 100 by dollar volume is used as the cash-alternative exposure in mixed states. When both signals are bullish, 100% goes to TQQQ; in mixed states, 50% goes to TQQQ and 50% to the top market-cap stock.
299.overfit_notes: The 20-day momentum and 200-day median are both standard. Using the top-1 market-cap stock as the non-leveraged allocation (instead of BIL) is a non-canonical design that benefits from large-cap bias in the backtest period. Mild overfit from this vehicle selection.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: One condition true → 50% TQQQ / 50% top-cap stock; neither true → 100% top-cap stock

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 1 stock by market cap from top 100 by dollar volume

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest-cap stock

300.name: Dual Mom Top-1 Step-Down (cc3_301.py)
300.description: A four-state hybrid that combines the 200-day median, 20-day momentum, and 60-day momentum to drive a step-down allocation across TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and BIL. As the vote count drops from 3 to 0, the strategy steps down from pure TQQQ through mixed TQQQ/top-cap, to pure top-cap, and finally to a 50/50 top-cap/BIL split.
300.overfit_notes: Three components (median + two momentum periods) at near-standard settings; the specific 4-state allocation plan mixing TQQQ, top-cap stock, and BIL in an asymmetric step-down is a highly specific design choice. The universe selection (top market cap stock) adds vehicle-selection risk.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ close > median of last 200 daily closes (1 vote)

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: QQQ 20-day return > 0 (1 vote); QQQ 60-day return > 0 (1 vote)

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: 3 votes → 100% TQQQ; 2 votes → 50% TQQQ / 50% top-cap; 1 vote → 100% top-cap; 0 votes → 50% top-cap / 50% BIL

rules.label: Universe
rules.text: Top 1 stock by market cap from top 100 by dollar volume

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest-cap stock / BIL
